title: 99 Problems

earnings.assumptions: Estimate reflects ongoing catalog streaming, recurrent licensing interest, and long-tail rap-catalog demand.

ownership.master_owner: Label-led master ownership with artist-side royalty and ownership leverage
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing appears split across writers and publishers
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No song-specific catalog sale adjustment is reflected
ownership.notes: Modeled gross-to-net estimate using catalog and streaming assumptions.

meaning_summary: This track pairs a memorable hook with strong cultural recall, which helps explain its staying power and long-tail commercial value.
revenue_drivers: Catalog streaming remains the main long-tail driver for recognizable rap tracks.
Playlist placement and cultural recall help the song stay active.
Sampling, sync use, and short-form rediscovery can extend earnings.
